admissibility_constraints: Projection must be independently motivated by the domain, not chosen only to force a contradiction.
The richer system must have a global section and the restricted system must be obstructed.
Forgotten structure must be the same structure that resolves the restricted obstruction.
Lossy projection without obstruction is not positive evidence.
Shared obstruction is not positive evidence.
Incomplete site maps are boundary cases, not failed positive instances.

forgotten_structure: branch support for divergent path names
analyses.hostile_reason: Git merge is a developer workflow, not a physics no-go theorem.
analyses.hypothesis_signal: supports H1 and weakly pushes against H0
analyses.independent_projection_basis: forget rename detection and semantic merge driver, retain only path-level equality constraints
analyses.intended_reading: A rename-aware merge has a finite global section, while the path-only projection creates a local-to-global merge conflict.

morphism.interpretation: morphism is blocked by an explicit preservation failure
morphism.name: git_forget_rename_metadata

analyses.name: git_semantic_merge

analyses.hostile_reason: A safe migration can lose operational detail without producing an impossibility result.
analyses.hypothesis_signal: supports H4 constraint: loss alone is insufficient
analyses.independent_projection_basis: forget expand-contract rollout metadata, retain flat old/app/new schema compatibility
analyses.intended_reading: Projection loses migration detail, but both systems still have global sections. This should not count as PO1 support.

morphism.interpretation: morphism is blocked by an explicit preservation failure
morphism.name: database_forget_expand_contract_rollout

analyses.name: database_expand_contract

schema.boundary: projection may be lossy without producing a no-go obstruction
schema.name: database_expand_contract_schema

analyses.verdict: database_expand_contract: lossy projection, but no obstruction is created

analyses.hostile_reason: The richer policy object does not resolve the conflict; it only names it with more metadata.
analyses.hypothesis_signal: supports H4 constraint: inherited obstruction is insufficient
analyses.independent_projection_basis: flatten policy provenance and retain only effective allow/deny compatibility
analyses.intended_reading: Both rich and restricted policy systems are obstructed. This is inherited obstruction, not projection-created obstruction.

morphism.interpretation: morphism is blocked by an explicit preservation failure
morphism.name: access_control_forget_policy_metadata

schema.boundary: obstruction is inherited rather than created by projection

analyses.verdict: access_control_inheritance: obstruction is inherited, not projection-created

analyses.hostile_reason: Macro expansion introduces an intermediate syntactic category with no target site.
analyses.independent_projection_basis: attempt to erase macro-expansion phase and compare source/core/runtime only
analyses.intended_reading: The projection is not definable inside T26 because one source site has no target. This is a boundary, not PO1 support.

morphism.interpretation: morphism is blocked by an explicit preservation failure
morphism.name: type_system_forget_macro_phase

preserved_structure: source to core to runtime compilation intent

schema.boundary: site map incomplete or category change
schema.name: type_system_macro_expansion_schema

analyses.verdict: type_system_macro_expansion: projection not definable inside T26

hypotheses: H0: PO1 is domain-specific to GU/no-go style cases
H1: PO1 applies to at least one non-physics inherited domain
H2: PO1 applies across multiple unrelated domains with the same finite structure
H3: PO1 requires modification to handle non-physics domains
H4: PO1 is too broad or underconstrained to be useful

hypothesis_evaluations.verdict: At least one non-physics hostile case instantiates PO1.
PO1 applies to at least one non-physics domain in T30.
T30 does not show broad cross-domain generality; it shows one positive plus boundaries.
Some domains need richer site or syntax handling, but the finite machinery still classifies several negative controls.
PO1 is useful only with admissibility constraints; three-patch contradictions alone are too easy to manufacture.

po1_recommendation: Keep PO1 at partially_supported, but constrain it. T30 finds one non-physics positive instance, two negative controls, and one non-definable boundary. The schema is not merely GU-specific, but it is not yet broad enough to strengthen. Future uses should pass an admissibility check before being counted as PO1 evidence.
